stories.completionNotes: Reproducer landed at tests/pi/obdii/drive/test_dual_attribution_reproducer.py (new tests/pi/obdii/drive/ package dir). Clock-injected (InjectedClock patches datetime in drive.detector + drive.types) -- no time.sleep, no wall-clock dependency. Replays one physical leg (DRIVE_23_24_REPLAY, production thresholds: start 500RPM/10s, end 0RPM/60s) whose mid-leg OBD/ECU dropout trips _checkEcuSilenceDriveEnd (US-229 silence path) mid-drive while the engine never stops; the link recovers and -- with NO inter-drive continuation guard wired (MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS defined in types.py but UNUSED) -- a 2nd _startDrive mints drive_id #2. Harness drives DriveDetector.processValue (the lifecycle reading-delivery seam), registers the onDriveStart/onDriveEnd callbacks lifecycle uses, and reads DISTINCT drive_id off connection_log drive_start rows (real in-memory ObdDatabase, real drive_counter mint). Callback-observed emission set is cross-checked against connection_log inside the harness. Core test marked @pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True): pre-fix it reports xfailed (assertion 'DriveDetector emitted 2 drive_ids ([1,2])'; verified literal failure via --runxfail), keeping the default -m 'not slow' sweep GREEN; US-361 DELETES the one xfail decorator line to convert it to a live PASS regression net (its V-1 expects a plain pass). Second test (test_replayIsDeterministic_acrossTwoRuns) pins determinism (two independent runs produce identical attribution) and is fix-invariant. ruff clean; test 'call' time 0.38s (the ~17s wall time is pytest import/collection overhead, not the test). NO source code changed -- purely additive test-only story, no PM Rule 10 trigger. Atlas test-only reviewer sign-off to be recorded in the PR description by PM (per AC#6). bigDoDHash untouched (hash covers only validation.bigDefinitionOfDone per _freeze.canonicalizeBigDoD).

stories.goal: As a developer about to fix the DriveDetector dual-emission defect, I want a written RCA that names the file + line numbers in detector.py and lifecycle.py driving the second emission so that US-361 has a concrete fix target and Atlas has a load-bearing-subsystem RCA to gate against.
stories.completionNotes: RCA filed at offices/ralph/findings/2026-05-28-drive-detector-dual-attribution-rca.md (Ralph-equivalent path; offices/architect/ is Atlas's lane, off-limits to write -- AC#1 'or Ralph equivalent path'). Cross-linked TO Atlas's 2026-05-22 finding + the US-359 reproducer + Watch List A-9. KEY RESULT: git diff 77026b5 (pre-US-348 baseline) d6ad871 (V0.27.17 tip) -- src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py = +53/-0, EVERY line a comment/docstring/mod-history entry; ZERO executable-logic change. The US-349-add -> US-351-remove round-trip is logic-byte-identical to pre-US-349 (confirmed via git diff -w --ignore-blank-lines b729a5c d6ad871 on detector.py + lifecycle.py: only docstring text remains). HEAD detector.py == d6ad871 (chain-merge did not alter it), so RCA applies to the dispatched branch. CLASSIFICATION (AC#4): byte-identical-residual-race -- US-351 revert EXONERATED as cause; the residual is two pre-existing conditions the revert neither added nor removed. TWO MECHANISMS named with file:line: (A) single-process sequential split = the reproducer's path: _checkEcuSilenceDriveEnd (detector.py:899-947, called :554) fires drive_end mid-leg on an OBD dropout while the engine runs, then -- with MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS (types.py:54) DEFINED-BUT-UNUSED as a continuation guard -- a 2nd _startDrive (detector.py:692, _openDriveId :703/:1131) mints a new id; deterministic, present since US-229 (2026-04-23), independent of US-348/349/351; produces SEQUENTIAL non-overlapping ids. (B) two concurrent orchestrator processes = the PRODUCTION reality: drive_id is a PROCESS-GLOBAL singleton (drive_id.py:265-266; threading note :259-263), so a single process CANNOT produce the time-OVERLAPPING / 2x-cadence / RPM-1500-2000-apart-same-second rows Atlas observed -- overlap REQUIRES two processes each minting from the shared drive_counter (drive_id.py:211-231). lifecycle.py has a single _driveDetector per orchestrator (:1349) but NO cross-process single-instance guard (grep pidfile|flock|single.instance|already.running = none); plausible trigger is the US-354 deploy-hygiene class (old eclipse-obd not killed on deploy). US-361 RECOMMENDATION (matches frozen Q3 'both modules in scope, behavioral test'): defense-in-depth -- detector.py continuation guard for Mechanism A + lifecycle.py single-instance guard for Mechanism B + server tripwire (US-362/363) as the only backstop that CATCHES a residual Mechanism-B occurrence (a Pi-detector fix alone cannot prevent a 2nd process). US-361 commit footer should reference the RCA path (AC#5 / V-4). Evidence boundary documented: Mechanism B inferred from the process-global-singleton invariant + Atlas overlap/cadence evidence; raw 23/24 PID census not on dev box (US-360 conditionalOutcome) -- a journald PID census across 2026-05-22 14:43-14:47 on chi-eclipse-01 is the recommended IRL confirmation. Doc-only research story: no source changed, no pytest/lint gate applies. NOTE for PM: prior session's I-043 (bigDoDHash 251bad94 stored vs 5557ae5c computed) is very likely the SAME Windows-cp1252-encoding instrument artifact Atlas already diagnosed + dismissed in his 2026-05-28 Rule 13 sign-off (every -> arrow mojibakes under cp1252, making all 103 elements appear to differ); does NOT block Ralph story execution -- routed a verify-recommendation to PM inbox.

stories.goal: As a developer closing the F-107 data-integrity defect, I want a root-cause fix to the Pi-side DriveDetector + drive lifecycle (both src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py AND src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py in scope per Q3 ruling) that prevents dual-emission of the same physical leg, validated behaviorally via the US-359 reproducer-fixture-passes-with-1-emission criterion.
stories.completionNotes: Defense-in-depth landed across BOTH modules per US-360 RCA §6 + frozen Q3 ruling. MECHANISM A (detector.py, LIVE + validated): ECU-silence drive_end is now TENTATIVE not terminal. _checkEcuSilenceDriveEnd (detector.py) captures the closed drive_id + time BEFORE _endDrive clears the context; a new helper _isEcuSilenceContinuation() decides at _startDrive whether to RE-ATTACH to the prior id (setCurrentDriveId) vs mint (_openDriveId): re-attach when an ECU-silence marker is live AND the engine resumed (RPM back above start threshold, measured via _aboveThresholdSince -- the real engine-off interval) within MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS (5s; the previously-defined-but-UNUSED constant the RCA named, now imported + used). _clearEcuSilenceMarker() consumes the marker at every drive start; reset() clears it too. CRITICAL design point that keeps US-229 + US-311 green: ONLY the ECU-silence path arms the marker -- RPM-debounce ends (engine confirmed off via RPM=0) and forceKeyOff() never do, so genuine new drives always mint fresh. The US-311 warm-restart e2e (3 cycles, RPM-debounce ends, driveEndDurationSeconds=0.0 which disables the silence check entirely) and all US-229 silence unit tests are untouched. V-1: US-359 reproducer xfail marker DELETED -> plain PASS, exactly 1 drive_id on the Drive 23/24 replay (was [1,2]; verified RED->GREEN: pre-fix --runxfail showed 'emitted 2 drive_ids ([1,2])'). MECHANISM B (NEW src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/single_instance.py SingleInstanceGuard pidfile lock + injectable processIsAlive liveness seam; wired into lifecycle.py _initializeAllComponents step-0 + released last in _shutdownAllComponents; _singleInstanceGuard attr added to core.__init__ + mixin decl): the PRODUCTION drives-23/24 evidence is two CONCURRENT orchestrator processes (drive_id is a process-global singleton -> a single process cannot produce the observed time-overlap; RCA §4), which a detector fix alone cannot prevent. Guard makes a 2nd concurrent eclipse-obd refuse to start. Shipped DEFAULT-OFF (pi.runtime.singleInstanceGuard.enabled defaults False) -- DELIBERATE: (a) the boot path is exercised by 36 integration test files so default-off = zero regression risk; (b) enabling-in-prod is a load-bearing change that should ride an Atlas Rule 10 sign-off; (c) genuine design question -- the real trigger is the US-354 deploy-hygiene class (old proc not killed), where pidfile-refuse makes the NEW deployed proc refuse while the STALE one survives (maybe wrong winner); the server-side attribution_anomaly tripwire (US-362/363) is the real-time backstop per the RCA. Routed the enable-now-vs-keep-dark ruling to PM+Atlas (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-28-from-rex-us361-done-mechanism-b-default-off.md) rather than decide unilaterally in a headless run. GATES: pytest tests/pi/ -m 'not slow' = 1530 passed / 16 skipped / 0 failed (V-2/V-3 Pi side + AC#3); pytest tests/server/ -m 'not slow' = 806 passed / 12 skipped / 0 failed (V-3/AC#4 B-104 compute path clean); new guard tests = 10 passed; ruff clean on all 7 files; V-4 grep = zero drive_statistics imports/CREATE in src/pi/ (only retirement/DROP machinery + historical comments -- table stays retired, AC#5). REMAINING CROSS-AGENT GATES (Ralph cannot self-satisfy, routed to PM): AC#6/V-5 Atlas Rule 10 sign-off on architecture.md §10.7 amendment is owned by US-373 (pending) + Atlas; AC#7 commit footer 'RCA: offices/ralph/findings/2026-05-28-drive-detector-dual-attribution-rca.md' is PM's at commit time. Marked passes:true per the in-sprint precedent (US-359/US-360 both passes:true with PM-recorded sign-off notes); all runnable validation is GREEN.

stories.goal: As a server-side analytics consumer of raw realtime_data, I want a detect_overlapping_drives(session, drive_id) -> list[int] helper that returns the set of other drive_ids overlapping the given drive_id by any second of time-range intersection so that downstream compute paths (US-363 + US-364) have a SSOT overlap detector.
stories.completionNotes: SSOT overlap detector landed at src/server/analytics/overlap.py: detect_overlapping_drives(session, drive_id: int) -> list[int] (AC#1, exact signature). Reads each drive's [min(timestamp), max(timestamp)] window from RAW realtime_data via a single grouped query (func.min/func.max GROUP BY drive_id, WHERE drive_id IS NOT NULL AND != target) -- NEVER drive_summary derived rows (AC#3), matching the drive_summary_compute.py MIN/MAX pattern so it cannot inherit a baked-in attribution error. Overlap = strict time-range intersection per second (AC#2): private _rangesOverlapBySecond floors both endpoints to the whole second (_floorToSecond) then does inclusive interval compare (aStart<=bEnd and bStart<=aEnd), so any single shared second (incl. one drive ending the same second another begins, and sub-second-same-second cases) = match; ε=0 hardcoded (no clock-jitter slack) -- nonzero ε deliberately NOT a parameter per conditionalOutcome 3 (separate Story w/ empirical justification). Pure query, zero DB writes -> idempotent (AC#5; asserted by test_isPureQuery_noDbWrites: row count unchanged after two calls). Google-style docstring states per-second intersection + ε=0 default (AC#6/V-4). TESTS (AC#4): tests/server/analytics/test_overlap.py, 10 tests, real in-memory SQLite + real ORM + real realtime_data INSERTs (no seam mocks, post-I-040 discipline). V-1 pairwise drives 23+24 (F-107 same-physical-leg window 14:43-14:46 replicated from F-107 constants per conditionalOutcome 1; raw 23/24 telemetry not on dev box) -> [24]; V-2 drive 25 + 1-min-gap neighbor -> []; V-3 3-way 30/31/32 -> [31,32] AND transitively (31)->[30,32], (32)->[30,31], sorted; V-4 docstring assertion. Plus boundary cases: shared-boundary-second -> overlap; 1-second-gap -> no overlap; sub-second-same-second -> overlap (proves the floor logic, fails without it); target-with-no-rows -> []; NULL drive_id rows ignored. RED verified (ModuleNotFoundError pre-impl), GREEN 10/10. GATES: ruff clean on both files; pytest tests/server/ -m 'not slow' = 816 passed / 12 skipped / 0 failed (no regressions). V-5 (run helper against production realtime_data snapshot on chi-srv-01: drives 23+24 overlap, 25+ don't) is an IRL chi-srv-01 step Ralph cannot run from the Windows dev box -- DEFERRED to the sprint validationMethod IRL drill; unit fixtures replicate the window per conditionalOutcome 1. Additive story (new module + new test only): no existing source touched, no PM Rule 10 trigger, bigDoDHash untouched. Consumers US-363 (compute tripwire) + US-364 (backfill) import via 'from src.server.analytics.overlap import detect_overlapping_drives'.

stories.goal: As a server-side compute path producing drive_summary + drive_statistics rows, I want to invoke detect_overlapping_drives and stamp data_quality='attribution_anomaly' on rows whose drive_id has any overlap so that the V0.27.18 dual-attribution pattern is observable downstream as a per-row flag. The tripwire is observability not refusal: anomaly rows remain readable; consumers degrade gracefully.
stories.completionNotes: Tripwire wired into BOTH server compute paths + shared v0010 migration. SCHEMA (AC#1): drive_summary had NO data_quality column at all (the AC's 'extend enum on both tables' wording assumed one existed) -- so this story ADDs the column to drive_summary AND extends the existing drive_statistics CHECK. ORM (src/server/db/models.py): new shared SSOT constant DATA_QUALITY_ATTRIBUTION_ANOMALY='attribution_anomaly'; DriveSummary gains data_quality VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'full' + CheckConstraint ck_drive_summary_data_quality (enum {full, attribution_anomaly} -- drive_summary has no sample-count notion so sparse/below_threshold are deliberately NOT allowed there) + Index idx_drive_summary_data_quality; DriveStatistic enum tuple DRIVE_STATISTICS_DATA_QUALITY_VALUES extended to {full, sparse, below_threshold, attribution_anomaly}. GOTCHA handled: drive_statistics_compute.py had an import-time assertion pinning the enum to exactly the 3 sample-count buckets -- updated it to the 4-value set (added DATA_QUALITY_ANOMALY alias) so imports don't break. COMPUTE (AC#2/#3): compute_drive_summary calls detect_overlapping_drives(session, driveId) (US-362 SSOT) -> data_quality='attribution_anomaly' when overlap, else 'full'. compute_drive_statistics applies the SAME detector ONCE per drive before the per-parameter loop; when overlap, EVERY parameter row is stamped 'attribution_anomaly', OVERRIDING the Atlas Refinement B sample-count classification (the whole drive's attribution is suspect, not one PID). Both paths log a WARNING on anomaly. Idempotent (AC#6): detector is a pure deterministic query over the same realtime_data -> re-run yields identical data_quality (asserted by test_anomalyIsIdempotent_acrossRecompute on both paths). MIGRATION (AC#1): NOTE -- repo uses the explicit MigrationRunner registry (TD-029 Path B), not literal Alembic; the sprint/PRD say 'Alembic v0010' loosely. v0010 = src/server/migrations/versions/v0010_us363_attribution_anomaly_data_quality.py, the SINGLE shared V0.28.0 migration; apply() is structured as ordered substep fns so US-365/368/370/371/372 append their own calls (clearly marked insertion point). US-363 substep (a) ADD COLUMN+CHECK+INDEX on drive_summary (mirrors v0009 shape + per-piece INFORMATION_SCHEMA idempotency probes + post-condition probe); substep (b) DROP+re-ADD the drive_statistics CHECK to widen the enum (MariaDB can't widen in place), idempotent via CHECK_CLAUSE probe (skips if 'attribution_anomaly' already present -> covers fresh create_all + re-run). stories.completionNotes: Server-side vehicle_info ECU-lineage schema landed (additive, server-only). ORM (src/server/db/models.py VehicleInfo): 5 new columns -- ecu_signature TEXT NOT NULL, cal_signature TEXT NULL, ecu_install_timestamp_utc DATETIME NOT NULL, ecu_removal_timestamp_utc DATETIME NULL, notes TEXT NULL (AC#1/#4) -- plus a STORED generated marker ecu_active_marker + UniqueConstraint uq_vehicle_info_single_active. SINGLE-ACTIVE ENFORCEMENT (AC#2): MariaDB has NO partial unique index (the recon claim of 'UNIQUE INDEX ... WHERE' was WRONG -- MySQL/MariaDB UNIQUE permits multiple NULLs), so per US-365 conditionalOutcome the chosen mechanism is a STORED generated marker = (CASE WHEN ecu_removal_timestamp_utc IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) carrying a UNIQUE index: value 1 must be unique => <=1 active row; NULL repeats freely (closed rows unconstrained). Declared in the ORM (create_all owns it; SQLite 3.31+ supports STORED generated cols + the unique index, so V-2/V-3 are real unit tests -- insert active when none -> ok; insert 2nd active -> IntegrityError) AND added in the v0010 migration for existing prod tables, so NO ORM-vs-migration divergence. DATETIME (not TIMESTAMP) matches the schema's other *_utc cols (synced_at/created_at) and dodges the TIMESTAMP epoch range; AC's 'TIMESTAMP' is the generic term. MIGRATION (AC#6): new substep _applyVehicleInfoEcuColumns in v0010 at the marked insertion point, AFTER the US-371 rename and BEFORE any US-370 substep (US-370 FKs vehicle_info.ecu_signature -- Atlas Refinements row 16 ordering). Idempotent per the v0009/US-363 probe pattern: each ADD COLUMN guarded by a column probe, the unique index by an index probe; the legacy backfill + the two MODIFY ... NOT NULL run ONLY when ecu_signature was absent at entry (freshAdd gate), so a fully-migrated/fresh-create_all DB is a PURE no-op (test_migratedIsPureNoOp + the existing TestApplyFullyMigrated alters==[] both stay green); post-condition probe asserts ecu_signature present. stories.completionNotes: Three F-108 ECU-lineage CLIs landed (additive, server-only) over the US-365 schema. SHARED SUPPORT (src/server/cli/_ecu_lineage_support.py): resolveSyncDatabaseUrl (async->sync URL swap, copied from recompute_drive_analytics rather than importing its private fn -- decoupling, ~10 dup lines justified per CLAUDE.md helper rule); ecuLineageColumnsPresent(engine) (SQLAlchemy inspect probe -> graceful pre-v0010 error instead of a missing-column OperationalError crash); getActiveVehicleInfo(session) (the single ecu_removal_timestamp_utc IS NULL row); nextSourceId(session, device) (max+1 within a source_device namespace -- see SOURCE-ID note below); parseIsoTimestamp (3.11 fromisoformat, 'Z'/offset accepted, normalised to NAIVE UTC to match the *_utc DateTime cols + be comparable to stored installs); appendNote (pure, deterministic, unit-tested) formatting an append-only '[<utc-iso>] <text>' line. stamp_ecu_swap (AC#1/#3/#4/#7): close-prior+open-new in ONE transaction. CRITICAL atomicity detail -- the close UPDATE (sets removal => generated ecu_active_marker 1->NULL) is session.flush()'d BEFORE the new active row is added, because SQLAlchemy's unit-of-work groups INSERTs before UPDATEs by default and would otherwise momentarily have two marker=1 rows => uq_vehicle_info_single_active IntegrityError mid-flush; both statements stay in one commit so any failure rolls BOTH back and the single-active invariant holds (AC#4). Idempotency (AC#3, V-2/V-9): key = (active.signature, active.install) vs (--signature, --as-of); identical => INFO 'no-op'/'already stamped', exit 0, zero writes. No-silent-rewrite (V-3, conditionalOutcome 2): same signature + DIFFERENT --as-of => ERROR exit 2, state unchanged. Identity-immutability raise (AC#7/V-8, = US-365 AC#9 deferral): --update-existing-signature flag is REFUSED outright with a documented 'append-only; use close+open' error before any DB touch. Bootstrap (AC#5): no currently-active row => graceful ERROR pointing at the US-367 backfill script (stamp_ecu_swap has nothing to close => never bootstraps the first row). Bad --as-of => graceful ERROR. show_ecu_lineage (AC#2, V-4/V-6/V-12): pure reader, lists every row ordered by install asc, ACTIVE-marks the open row, prints 'no ECU lineage recorded yet' on empty (exit 0). add_ecu_note (AC#7, V-10/V-11): appends a timestamped line to the MUTABLE notes col (history-preserving; prior lines never overwritten -- notes is NOT an identity col); bogus --vehicle-info-id => 'vehicle_info id not found' ERROR, no partial state. SOURCE-ID note (no prior convention existed -- I set the precedent): server-authored lineage rows reuse the prior row's source_device and take nextSourceId (max+1 in that namespace) to satisfy UNIQUE(source_device, source_id); the Pi never authors these rows. WRITER-PATH note for US-368/PM: stamp_ecu_swap legitimately mutates ecu_removal_timestamp_utc (write-once close) and add_ecu_note mutates notes -- both via ORM attribute assignment, the SANCTIONED writer path. US-368's planned 'no UPDATE on vehicle_info' grep test must therefore target raw SQL 'UPDATE ... SET ecu_signature'/identity columns, NOT ORM attribute sets (which is exactly how the sanctioned close+open writes). GATES: 16 new tests across 4 files (8 stamp + 3 show + 4 add incl. 2 pure-helper + 1 named identity-immutability regression) RED-verified (ImportError pre-impl) then GREEN; pytest tests/server/ -m 'not slow' = 911 passed / 12 skipped / 0 failed (was 895 post-US-365; +16, no regressions); ruff clean on all 8 files (UP017 timezone.utc->UTC autofixed on touched files only). NO Atlas Rule 10 gate for US-366 (no schema change, no architecture.md touch -- the §5.X/§10.7 doc is US-373's deliverable; US-366 ACs do not list a Rule 10 sign-off, unlike US-361/363/365). AC#5 'pre-migration graceful error' tested via a hand-rolled legacy vehicle_info (VIN cols only) -> all three CLIs emit the shared PRE_MIGRATION_MESSAGE + non-zero/exit-1. AC#6 CIO+Spool usage docs live in each CLI module docstring (Usage:: blocks). Changes UNSTAGED per PM protocol.

stories.completionNotes: F-109 Pi->server sync + reader CLI landed across BOTH tiers, TDD throughout (RED verified per file before GREEN). CONTEXT: US-368 created the Pi+server dtc_freeze_frame tables but (a) left the Pi table OUT of the delta-sync registry and (b) the server table needed a cross-tier resolver -- US-369 closes both. PI (1 line + tests): registered 'dtc_freeze_frame':'id' in sync_log.PK_COLUMN (src/pi/data/sync_log.py) -> joins DELTA_SYNC_TABLES + IN_SCOPE_TABLES so the MIL_ON capture actually pushes (closes the V0.27.18 'captured but never synced' class for this table). SERVER SYNC (src/server/api/sync.py): dtc_freeze_frame is accepted at the payload boundary (ACCEPTED_TABLES = registry | {dtc_freeze_frame}) but is NOT a generic _TABLE_REGISTRY column-copy table -- its Pi rows carry a cross-tier shape (vehicle_info_vin TEXT + Pi-local dtc_log_id + a JSON-STRING pid_responses_json + Pi-only notes/data_source). New dedicated resolver _syncDtcFreezeFrameRows runs LAST in runSyncUpsert (after the generic loop) so any dtc_log/vehicle_info rows in the SAME batch are already upserted + resolvable. It: (1) resolves vehicle_info_vin -> integer vehicle_info_id via the Q4 TEMPORAL JOIN -- _resolveVehicleInfoIdForCapture selects the vehicle_info row whose [ecu_install, ecu_removal] window contains captured_at (removal NULL = open); the same VIN has TWO rows (prior + new ECU, same car) so the window is what disambiguates. UNRESOLVABLE vin (0 rows) or AMBIGUOUS (>1, windows overlap) => raise ValueError = FAIL LOUDLY, no silent re-resolve (conditionalOutcome 1). (2) resolves Pi-local dtc_log_id -> server dtc_log.id via (source_device, source_id) [_resolveServerDtcLogId]; unresolved => NULL + WARNING (graceful, fills on resync). (3) _coercePidResponses parses the JSON string -> dict for the JSON column ({} on empty/unparseable). (4) idempotent (source_device, source_id) upsert via the existing _upsertBatch so a cursor-reset resync UPDATEs not duplicates, and the whole batch stays in ONE transaction (the reason sync does NOT call insertDtcFreezeFrame, which commits per-row + is insert-only; that writer-path remains the DIRECT-insert SSOT, and the window-resolution structurally enforces the same temporal invariant). The FK is bound ONCE at sync time by capture-time window -> later stamp_ecu_swap rows never re-point an existing freeze-frame (V-3 Q4 round-trip). NEW notes COLUMN: server DtcFreezeFrame model + the v0010 CREATE TABLE DDL gained `notes TEXT` (US-368 left it Pi-only); synced verbatim so the degraded-capture gap explanation survives the round-trip and the CLI can surface it (conditionalOutcome 2). The dtc_freeze_frame table is brand-new this sprint (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, not yet deployed) so the DDL add is clean. CLI (src/server/cli/show_dtc_freeze_frame.py NEW): show_dtc_freeze_frame --dtc-log-id N (server dtc_log.id) prints the DTC (code+status+first_seen), the freeze-frame captured_at + sorted 16-PID dict, and the joined vehicle_info row (ecu_signature/cal/install/removal) via the STORED FK -- pure reader, reuses resolveSyncDatabaseUrl from _ecu_lineage_support. Graceful: no freeze-frame -> 'no freeze-frame recorded for this DTC' exit 0 (V-4/AC#5); empty pid_responses_json -> 'freeze-frame captured but Mode 02 PIDs unavailable' + notes (conditionalOutcome 2); multiple frames for one dtc_log_id -> most recent by captured_at + WARNING (conditionalOutcome 3). GATES: pytest tests/server/ -m 'not slow' = 948 passed/12 skip/0 fail (+ new test_sync_dtc_freeze_frame 10 + test_show_dtc_freeze_frame 5; updated test_sync.py::test_allAcceptedTables for the 12th table); pytest tests/pi/ -m 'not slow' = 1549 passed/16 skip/0 fail (+ new test_sync_log_dtc_freeze_frame 4; updated test_sync_log_pk_variants EXPECTED_PK_COLUMN + count 9->10; updated test_sync_client_upsert _createAllInScopeStubs with a dtc_freeze_frame stub since adding the table to DELTA_SYNC_TABLES made getDeltaRows iterate it); seed_pi_fixture + 2 integration sync tests GREEN (they build stubs dynamically from IN_SCOPE_TABLES, auto-adapt); ruff clean on all 8 touched files. DEV-RUNNABLE VALIDATION ALL GREEN via in-memory SQLite + real ORM (no seam mocks, post-I-040): V-1 sync round-trip preserves 16 PIDs + resolves both FKs; V-2 CLI prints DTC+16 PIDs+ecu_signature; V-3 Q4 binds to capture-time ECU not later swap; V-4 missing freeze-frame exit 0; V-5 dtc_freeze_frame in ACCEPTED_TABLES + JSON preserved. IRL-ONLY (deferred to sprint validationMethod drill, not runnable from Windows dev box, same precedent as US-362 V-5 / US-363): the ACTUAL Pi->chi-srv-01 sync of a real freeze-frame + show_dtc_freeze_frame against prod obd2db. ROUTED to PM inbox: (a) US-369 added a `notes` column to dtc_freeze_frame -> US-373 §5.X should document the FINAL landed dtc_freeze_frame shape incl. notes (US-373 conditionalOutcome 'document FINAL landed state'); (b) IRL freeze-frame sync round-trip belongs on the sprint IRL-drill checklist. No PM Rule 10 / Atlas gate is named in US-369's own ACs (unlike US-365/368). Changes UNSTAGED per PM protocol.

RATIFIED DEVIATION FROM FROZEN AC#1/VC#1: frozen wording says 'ecu_signature FK -> vehicle_info'; Atlas option-(c) (2026-05-29, post-freeze) ruled NO FK -- ecu_signature is speed_pid_calibration's OWN UNIQUE natural key (VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL UNIQUE), because the correction factor is window-invariant (a property of the ECU signature, stable across install windows); a FK to vehicle_info.id binds it to one window, and a UNIQUE on vehicle_info.ecu_signature breaks the US-365 append-only invariant. Built to the ruling, not the frozen FK wording (same precedent as US-363 AC#1 / US-372 source_id). SCHEMA (AC#1): new ORM model SpeedPidCalibration (src/server/db/models.py) -- id PK, ecu_signature VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL + UNIQUE (uq_speed_pid_calibration_ecu_signature), correction_factor DOUBLE/Float NOT NULL, capture_method VARCHAR(32) + CHECK enum {gps_correlation,gear_math,vendor_spec,default} (F-076 §1 ENUM realized as VARCHAR+CHECK for SQLite parity, matching the data_quality precedent; NULL-allowed), captured_at_timestamp_utc DATETIME, captured_by VARCHAR(128), provenance TEXT NOT NULL, notes TEXT. SSOT constants exported from models.py (table/length/unique-name/check-name/enum-tuple/default-factor/empirical-prefix). MIGRATION (AC#1/AC#4): v0010 _applySpeedPidCalibrationTable substep wired at the reserved '# ---- US-370 substep appends here ----' insertion point, AFTER _applyVehicleInfoEcuColumns (US-365-before-US-370 ordering preserved + documented in the substep docstring per Atlas Refinements row 16, even though option-(c) removed the FK -- the tables share the ecu_signature VALUE and US-367 is its source). New table -> CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + serverTableExists short-circuit + post-condition probe (US-368/v0005 pattern). SEED (AC#2/AC#3/VC#5): 2 rows via INSERT IGNORE (idempotent on the UNIQUE key -- covers re-run + partial-success recovery + create_all-then-migrate): prior ECU MD346675 factor=1.0 provenance='gear-math-drive-18-3rd-gear-fit'; new ECU MD335287 factor=0.5 provenance='rough-seed-drive-26-gear-math' notes contains 'INITIAL ESTIMATE pending Spool Q2 GPS-correlation refinement'. capture_method='gear_math' for BOTH seeds (the frozen provenance strings both say 'gear-math'; the 0.5 IS a rough Drive-26 gear-math estimate, not a pure default -- 'gear_math' is the honest method tag; CO#2's 'default' framing was for a no-basis path, but we have a Drive-26 basis). Q1 ruling 2026-05-28: backfill from source_id + CHECK invariant; SSOT-purist drop deferred to V0.28+ broader normalization.
stories.completionNotes: drive_summary.drive_id<->source_id invariant landed: backfill (UPDATE-before-ALTER, BOTH directions) + DB CHECK + writer-path discipline. SCHEMA SSOT (src/server/db/models.py): 2 new constants DRIVE_SUMMARY_DRIVE_ID_CHECK_NAME='chk_drive_id_source_id' + DRIVE_SUMMARY_DRIVE_ID_CHECK_CLAUSE; new CheckConstraint added to DriveSummary.__table_args__. CRITICAL clause shape: '(drive_id IS NULL AND source_id IS NULL) OR (drive_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NOT NULL AND drive_id = source_id)' -- the NOT-NULL guards are LOAD-BEARING: a bare 'drive_id = source_id' evaluates to NULL (not FALSE) under SQL 3-valued logic when exactly one side is NULL, and a CHECK PASSES on NULL, so the asymmetric smell row (source_id set, drive_id NULL) would slip through without them (caught in TDD: test_driveIdNullWithSourceIdSetRejected initially DID NOT RAISE). MIGRATION (v0010 _applyDriveSummaryDriveIdSourceIdInvariant, runs LAST in apply() -- depends on no other substep, only touches pre-v0010 columns): backfills BOTH directions BEFORE ADD CONSTRAINT (AC#1 step i drive_id<-source_id WHERE drive_id NULL AND source_id NOT NULL; conditionalOutcome 1 reverse source_id<-drive_id WHERE source_id NULL AND drive_id NOT NULL) so the constraint cannot fail on real data; idempotent early-return when the CHECK already exists (fresh create_all OR prior run -> no UPDATEs, no ALTER); a both-set-but-DIFFERENT row is NOT silently rewritten -> ADD CONSTRAINT fails loud (genuinely corrupt data surfaces). UPDATE-before-ALTER documented in substep docstring (AC#4). WRITER-PATH DISCIPLINE (AC#2, 3 server sites): (1) sync.py runSyncUpsert -- for drive_summary, mirror serverRow['drive_id']=source_id when absent (the Pi sends only `id`->source_id; without this the mirror inserts NULL and violates the new CHECK; conditionalOutcome 2 'write one column = both'); (2) drive_summary_compute.compute_drive_summary -- heal drive_id/source_id to driveId before flush (a legacy Pi-sync row arrives source_id-set + mirror-NULL); (3) analysis._ensureDriveSummary UPDATE branch -- heal source_id when NULL (its post-US-200 INSERT branch already set all three equal). KEY SCOPE CORRECTION (prior-session note was wrong): the Pi-side src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py table has NO source_id column at all (PK=drive_id only; wire renames drive_id->id->server source_id), so 'set both on Pi-side' (AC#2) + 'Pi writer for a new drive: drive_id=source_id' (V-5) are NOT literally applicable to the Pi -- the drive_id==source_id invariant is a SERVER-SIDE constraint established at the sync boundary. No Pi file touched; Pi suite GREEN unchanged. TESTS (TDD, RED-verified): NEW tests/server/test_drive_summary_drive_id_invariant.py (21 tests): ORM constants/constraint, SQLite enforcement (both-NULL allowed; equal allowed; asymmetric NULL rejected V-2; divergent rejected V-3), migration DDL shapes + substep (backfill-before-ADD ordering, idempotent no-op, table-missing/post-probe/add-failure raises, docstring), writer-path (V-5 sync mirrors source_id->drive_id; V-4 compute preserves invariant; compute heals a PRAGMA-seeded legacy NULL-mirror row). UPDATED 2 stale assertions that encoded the pre-US-372 smell (drive_id IS None on a Pi-sync row -- now == source_id): test_drive_summary_sync_update_propagation.py + test_drive_statistics_writer_post_us326.py pre-conditions. UPDATED 3 v0010 migration tests for the new substep: test_emitsExactlyTheExpectedAlters appends ADD_DRIVE_SUMMARY_DRIVE_ID_CHECK_DDL + the 2 state-scripting helpers script the chk_drive_id_source_id probe; dtc_freeze_frame + us371 apply()-tests script the probe as no-op. GATES: pytest tests/server/ -m 'not slow' = 981 collected, 0 failed (exit 0, all dots+skips; +21 new); pytest tests/pi/ -m 'not slow' = exit 0, 0 failed (cross-tier sync contract intact, no Pi file touched); ruff clean on all 11 touched files. CROSS-AGENT GATE NOT SELF-SATISFIABLE (routed to PM): AC#5 Atlas Rule 10 sign-off + the drive_summary CHECK entry in architecture.md S5.X are owned by US-373 + Atlas -- PENDING (same in-sprint precedent as US-361/363/365/368). ROUTED to PM inbox: (a) AC#2/V-5 'Pi-side set both' is N/A -- Pi drive_summary has no source_id column; US-373 S5.X should document the FINAL landed shape = server-side invariant + Pi single-column PK; (b) prior-session inbox note's 'Pi writer carried source_id since US-206' was a misread -- corrected here. Marked passes:true per the in-sprint precedent (all runnable validation GREEN; cross-agent Atlas gate routed). Changes UNSTAGED per PM protocol.
